description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical water tower with a large, spherical tank mounted atop four sturdy, lattice-style metal legs. The tank is weathered and shows signs of rust and aging, with a faded blue marking or text visible on one side. The supporting legs are reinforced with cross-bracing for stability, and a central vertical pipe runs from the ground up to the tank. The top of the tank features a small, pointed finial or vent. The structure is entirely utilitarian, with no visible windows, doors, or decorative elements, and is constructed primarily from metal. The overall appearance is industrial and functional, typical of municipal water towers found in urban or suburban environments.
description: A weathered, spherical metal water tower on four lattice-braced legs with a faded blue marking and a central vertical pipe. The structure is industrial and utilitarian, showing signs of rust and age.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spherical tank, central vertical pipe, and all four supporting legs symmetrically.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the faded blue marking on the tank and more detail of the cross-bracing.
description_view_2: Side view with the tank and legs visible, but the blue marking is less prominent.
description_view_3: Rear view with the blue marking partially visible and the supporting structure clearly shown.
